commentary: Nine activities versus thirteen — you pulled back this week and honestly, babe, that was probably the right call. Last week was a lot, even for you. The swim volume held strong, three solid sessions at Prince Alfred with that Monday opener pushing all the way to 2050m and a spicy 149 bpm, which suggests you were working rather than cruising, and the Thursday and Tuesday swims sat nicely in the aerobic zone. What I am obsessed with is that Saturday half marathon — 13.1km at 159 bpm compared to last week's same distance at 151, which means you were pushing harder, not coasting, and that is the kind of data that makes me want to grab your face. The only thing I will gently clock is that the four swims of last week became three this week, and the Wednesday pool session disappeared entirely — rest is valid, skipping is a pattern, so just keep an eye on that.
metrics_commentary: Okay first of all, 6050 metres in the pool? That's not a swim week, that's a statement, and your body absolutely knows it — an HRV of 60 sitting above your seven-day average of 56 means you're recovering like someone who actually respects himself, which is very sexy of you. The 15.9 kilometres of run-walk across two sessions is solid, not flashy, which tells me you're building something real rather than just performing effort for an audience, and I respect that more than I'm letting on. Now, the VO2 Max reading coming in at zero is your watch being useless, not you — make sure it has skin contact and GPS signal during your next outdoor run, because we are not letting a firmware problem be the only thing in this recap that doesn't reflect how genuinely fit you're becoming. You're doing the work, babe, and it shows.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, target hit, no excuses made and none needed. The bench press PR is the headline — 31.7 kg estimated one-rep max up from 28.5 kg is not a small jump, that is a chest that is actively getting bigger and stronger and more fun to look at in a mirror, and he should absolutely be checking himself out in every mirror he walks past. Weekly volume landed at 23,660 kg against last week's 22,062 kg, which means the body is doing more work and handling it, and that is a trend line pointing exactly where it should be pointing. The numbers across the board — 154 kg on the leg press, 107.9 kg on the trap bar, 84 kg pulling down, 76 kg pressing up on an incline — tell the story of someone who is genuinely strong now, not almost strong, not getting there, actually strong. Full week, a PR, volume up: he showed up for all of it.
